Grammarly Review: AI Writing Assistant for Perfect Content

Every email, proposal, and piece of content you send as a solopreneur reflects your professionalism. Grammarly ensures that reflection is always polished, clear, and effective.

What Grammarly Actually Does

Grammarly isn't just a spell checker. It's an AI-powered writing assistant that analyzes:

  • Spelling and grammar: The basics
  • Punctuation: Commas, semicolons, etc.
  • Clarity: Sentence structure, readability
  • Engagement: Vocabulary variety
  • Delivery: Tone, formality, confidence
  • Plagiarism: Original content verification

Free vs. Premium: What's Different

What Free Includes

  • Spelling errors
  • Grammar mistakes
  • Basic punctuation
  • Conciseness suggestions
  • Basic tone detection

Verdict: Excellent for casual use. Catches embarrassing errors.

What Premium Adds

  • Clarity improvements
  • Vocabulary enhancements
  • Tone adjustments
  • Formality level
  • Plagiarism detection
  • Style guide (teams)
  • Sentence variety

Verdict: Essential for professional writers and serious content creators.

The Real Value for Solopreneurs

1. Professional Emails

Every client communication matters. Grammarly ensures:

  • No typos in proposals
  • Appropriate tone
  • Clear instructions
  • Confident language

2. Content Marketing

For blog posts and articles:

  • Improved readability scores
  • Vocabulary variety
  • Engaging sentence structure
  • Plagiarism-free guarantee

3. Sales Copy

Grammarly helps:

  • Maintain confident tone
  • Remove hedging language
  • Improve persuasion (clarity helps)

4. Social Media

Even short posts:

  • Catch autocorrect fails
  • Maintain brand voice
  • Emoji and punctuation help

Pricing Plans

Plan Price Features
Free $0 Spelling, grammar, basic clarity
Premium $12/month (annual) All features, plagiarism checker
Business $15/month/seat Team features, style guide

Tip: Annual Premium saves ~40% vs. monthly.

How Grammarly Works

Browser Extension

Works in:

  • Gmail
  • Google Docs
  • Notion
  • Twitter/X
  • LinkedIn
  • Any web text field

Desktop App

Full word processor with Grammarly built-in.

Mobile Keyboard

iOS and Android keyboards with Grammarly built-in.

MS Word/Outlook Add-in

For office suites, direct integration.

The Best Features

Tone Detection

See how your writing comes across:

  • Formal
  • Confident
  • Friendly
  • Optimistic
  • Urgent

Adjust based on audience.

Clarity Score

Real-time readability score:

  • 90-100: Easily understood by 5th grader
  • 60-70: Requires high school education
  • Below 60: Difficult for most readers

Aim for 70-80 for professional content.

Goals

Set specific goals:

  • Audience (knowledgeable, general)
  • Style (formal, neutral)
  • Emotion (mild to strong)
  • Intent (inform, convince)

Receive tailored suggestions.

Plagiarism Checker

Scan your content against:

  • 16 billion web pages
  • Academic databases
  • Published works

Essential for content marketing and SEO.

Grammarly vs. Alternatives

Tool Price Best For
Grammarly Free $0 Most writers
Grammarly Premium $12/mo Professionals
ProWritingAid $20/mo Fiction authors
Hemingway Free-$10 Clarity focus
LanguageTool Free-$5 Privacy focus

Real ROI Example

Daily Use:

  • 20 emails checked
  • 1 blog post
  • Social media posts
  • 1 proposal/week

Errors caught monthly:

  • 50-100 typos/spelling
  • 30-50 grammar issues
  • 20-30 clarity improvements

Time saved: 2-3 hours/month avoiding rewrites

Money saved: Zero embarrassing client errors (priceless)

Setting Up Grammarly

Step 1: Install Everywhere

  • Browser extension (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge)
  • Desktop app for longer writing
  • Mobile keyboard for on-the-go

Step 2: Create Dictionary

Add words Grammarly should ignore:

  • Brand names
  • Industry terms
  • Common client names

Step 3: Set Goals

For different content types, set appropriate goals:

  • Client emails: Formal, confident
  • Blog posts: Neutral, engaging
  • Social media: Friendly, optimistic

Step 4: Review Weekly

Check your "Statistics" for:

  • Common mistakes
  • Areas to improve manually
  • Overall writing quality

Limitations and Considerations

It's Not Perfect

Grammarly can:

  • Be wrong (use your judgment)
  • Miss context-specific errors
  • Over-correct creative writing
  • Slow down some browsers

Doesn't Replace Human Editing

For major publications, still:

  • Get human editorial review
  • Read aloud for flow
  • Check facts separately

Privacy Considerations

Grammarly processes text in the cloud:

  • Not HIPAA compliant
  • Don't use for sensitive documents
  • Review privacy policy for teams

Tips for Maximum Value

  1. Don't Accept Everything: Consider each suggestion; you're the expert
  2. Use the Goals Feature: Tailor settings to each document type
  3. Check Weekly Stats: Identify personal patterns
  4. Add Custom Dictionary: Prevent constant flags on your terms
  5. Combine with Reading Aloud: Catch what Grammarly misses

Getting Started

  1. Sign up for Grammarly (free tier)
  2. Install browser extension
  3. Use for a week with all writing
  4. Evaluate what Premium would catch
  5. Upgrade if seeing valuable suggestions

Final Verdict

Rating: 4.5/5

Grammarly is essential for any solopreneur who writes professionally—which is all of us. Even the free tier prevents embarrassing mistakes.

For serious content creators, Premium is worth it for:

  • Clarity improvements
  • Tone detection
  • Plagiarism checking
  • Vocabulary enhancement

The $12/month (annual) investment prevents costly errors, improves communication, and elevates professionalism.

Start: Free tier today. Upgrade to Premium within 2 weeks once you see the value.

Note: We recommend tools based on actual usefulness for solopreneurs. Some links may be affiliate links at no cost to you.
